data.description:
## Context / Why All code landing on `main` goes through Gerrit's two-vote gate (LLM-Review + Verified). We want every such commit associated with a rebar ticket, enforced as part of the **Verified (CI)** leg — the only enforcement point that both blocks submit AND can resolve an ID against the live store. The `rebar-ticket:` commit trailer already exists but nothing parses it. CI does not apply to specialized branches (the `tickets` branch never goes through Gerrit), so this gates only code changes to `main`. ## What Add a `rebar verify-commit-ticket` check that extracts a ticket reference from a commit message and resolves it against the authoritative store via the standard config store logic (`tracker_dir`/`tickets_branch`/`tickets_remote`, overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` / env), then wire it as a fail-fast job in `gerrit-verify.yaml` so a missing/unresolvable ticket drives Verified-1. Accept alias, full ID, short ID, and Jira key (project-configured prefix) — all handled by the existing `require_id` / `resolve_ticket_id`. ## Decision: trailer vs first-element Canonical location is the **`rebar-ticket:` trailer**; a leading `:` subject token is also accepted. First-element-only enforcement is NOT adopted: it gives no significant fragility or performance benefit over the trailer (resolution is a single store lookup regardless of position; both are fixed-position parses), while the trailer is git-native, already in use, and does not constrain the 50-char subject. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] New `src/rebar/_commands/verify_commit.py`: `extract_ticket_refs(message) -> list[str]` (`rebar-ticket:` trailer, case-insensitive, value tokens split on whitespace/parens; plus a leading `:` subject token) and `verify_commit_message(message, *, root=None)` resolving each candidate via `_seam.require_id` / `resolve_ticket_id` against `config.tracker_dir(root)`; passes if ANY candidate resolves. - [ ] CLI `rebar verify-commit-ticket [--rev | --message-file

| --message ]` intercept in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (+ help in `src/rebar/_cli/_help.py`): reads the message, exit 0 if a candidate resolves, exit 1 with a clear diagnostic (what was tried + the fix) otherwise. Merge commits (>1 parent, when `--rev` given) are exempt (exit 0). When `verify.require_ticket_for_commit` is false, exit 0 (no-op / staged rollout). - [ ] Config: `verify.require_ticket_for_commit: bool = False` in `VerifyConfig` + `_SECTIONS['verify']` in `src/rebar/config.py` (env `REBAR_VERIFY_REQUIRE_TICKET_FOR_COMMIT` via the generic env layer); enabled for THIS project in `rebar.toml`. - [ ] Store resolution uses the standard config helpers and is overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` / `sync.remote` / `tracker.branch` — no bespoke store path. - [ ] CI: `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` gains a `require-ticket` job (needs `clear-vote`) that checks out the change, `pip install -e .`, mounts the authoritative tickets store from GitHub (`git fetch https://github.com/${{ github.repository }} tickets` → `git worktree add .tickets-tracker`), and runs `rebar verify-commit-ticket --rev HEAD`. `build-and-test` and `vote` depend on it so a failure fails the run → Verified-1 (fail-fast, before the matrix). - [ ] Tests `tests/unit/test_verify_commit_ticket.py`: extraction (trailer with parenthetical, leading id, multiple, none); resolution against a fixture store for full/short/alias/prefix and a Jira key via a `.bridge_state/bindings.json` fixture; config-gate off → pass; enabled + no ticket → exit 1; merge-commit exempt. - [ ] Docs: CONTRIBUTING.md + CLAUDE.md git-workflow (require a `rebar-ticket:` trailer or a leading `:`) + docs/config.md (the new flag). ## Verification - `pytest tests/unit/test_verify_commit_ticket.py -q` - `rebar verify-commit-ticket --message "x: no ticket"; echo $?` → 1 (when enabled) - `rebar verify-commit-ticket --message-file <(git show -s --format=%B HEAD); echo $?` → 0 (this repo's HEAD carries a `rebar-ticket:` trailer) ## Out of scope - Non-Gerrit paths (the tickets branch; direct GitHub pushes — already blocked by the ruleset). - An advisory client-side `commit-msg` hook (optional future nicety). - A `rebar-ticket: none` bypass sentinel (kept strict; merge commits are the only exemption).
data.title: CI Verified gate: require a resolvable rebar ticket ID in every Gerrit commit to main
